title: Revenue Inflation Due to Join Explosion
description: Calculate total revenue per customer by joining orders and order_items. Each order can have multiple items. Ensure that revenue is not inflated due to duplicate joins.
sql_solution: SELECT o.customer_id, SUM(i.price) revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items i ON o.order_id = i.order_id GROUP BY o.customer_id;
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *

order_revenue = (
    orders.alias("o")
    .join(order_items.alias("i"), col("o.order_id") == col("i.order_id"), "inner")
    .select(
        col("o.customer_id").alias("customer_id"),
        col("i.price").alias("price")
    )
)

result = order_revenue.groupBy("customer_id").agg(sum("price").alias("revenue"))
